convention_proposal.title: @origin(...) presence convention v1
rule_when_required: EXISTING .hexa file with substantive change (>= 10 LOC of new logic, e.g. new function, new code path, schema change) — REQUIRED
Witness JSON spawned from another cycle's carry-forward — REQUIRED in either 'parent_cycle' top-level field OR an '@origin' annotation in body
rule_when_optional: Drive-by patches < 10 LOC, single-purpose (e.g. macOS date(1) cascade fix) — OPTIONAL if inline patch-site comment cites the audit-cycle slug
Config-line edits to registry .hexa files (state/kick/registry/backends/*) with inline relocation note — OPTIONAL
Refactors that don't change semantics (rename, formatting) — NOT REQUIRED
convention_proposal.placement: directly under @tool / @usage block, before any code or fn declarations (top-30-lines rule for grep audit)
convention_proposal.chained_extensions: for files extended by multiple subsequent ω-cycles, append a NEW @origin block per extension cycle (gold-standard: modules/mc_integrate/cli.hexa with 3 chained @origin blocks)
convention_proposal.non_witness_spawned_modifications: purely routine maintenance (typo, lint, version bump) does NOT require @origin. Drive-by patches that emerge from an audit cycle SHOULD cite that audit at the patch site (inline comment) even if no header @origin is added.
